claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for? If yes, how many children are you asking about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child I'm asking about. Their name is Alex and they're 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have one child, Alex, who is 8 years old.

Now I need to ask: do you currently live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great! And do you live with Alex, or are you paying towards their upkeep (such as for food, clothes, or pocket money) at least the same amount as Child Benefit would provide?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't live with me, but I do contribute towards their upkeep. I help out with things like clothes and school expenses - I give about £26 a week for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Just to clarify - Alex lives with someone else but you contribute approximately £26 per week towards their upkeep for things like clothes and school expenses.

Can you tell me: is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for clarifying that. One more important question: how long has it been since Alex stopped living with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'd prefer not to go into the personal details of when exactly that changed, but Alex hasn't been living with me for a while now. Is that relevant to whether I can claim?

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I understand, and I appreciate you sharing what you're comfortable with. Yes, the timing is relevant to eligibility in your situation.

According to the rules, when a child goes to live with someone else, you can usually continue to get Child Benefit for 8 weeks after they leave. After that initial 8-week period, you can continue to receive Child Benefit if you're contributing towards your child's upkeep by at least the same amount as the Child Benefit payment.

Since you mentioned Alex "hasn't been living with me for a while now," I need to understand if this means it's been more than 8 weeks. Could you confirm whether it's been more or less than 8 weeks since Alex stopped living with you? This will help me give you an accurate assessment of your eligibility.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Oh, yes - it's been much longer than 8 weeks. Alex has been living elsewhere for quite some time now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. Since it's been much longer than 8 weeks, the key factor for your eligibility is whether your contributions meet the threshold.

You mentioned you contribute about £26 per week towards Alex's upkeep. To confirm your eligibility, I need to verify: have you been making these contributions (or similar contributions) consistently throughout the period since Alex stopped living with you? 

Also, are you making these contributions weekly, or do you sometimes pay monthly or in lump sums?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I've been contributing regularly. I pay weekly - £26 a week consistently for Alex's needs.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Just one final question to complete the assessment:

Is Alex currently in hospital, in care, or in any residential accommodation provided by the local council?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in hospital or in care or anything like that. They're just living with someone else - nothing to do with the council or medical reasons.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Good News!

Based on the information you've provided, **you are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's why:

✓ **Alex meets the age requirement** - At 8 years old, Alex is under 16
✓ **You meet the residency requirement** - You live in the UK
✓ **You meet the responsibility requirement** - Even though Alex doesn't live with you, you're contributing £26 per week consistently towards Alex's upkeep (for clothes and school expenses), which meets or exceeds the Child Benefit payment amount
✓ **No one else is claiming** - Nobody else is currently receiving Child Benefit for Alex
✓ **No special circumstances** - Alex is not in hospital, care, or residential accommodation

According to the rules, when a child lives with someone else, you can continue to receive Child Benefit beyond the initial 8-week period as long as you contribute to your child's upkeep by at least the same amount as the Child Benefit payment. Your £26 weekly contribution meets this threshold.

**Important note:** If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge. You can still claim Child Benefit and use the Child Benefit tax calculator to work out if you need to pay this charge.

Would you like any additional information about how to make your claim?

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old (under 16), so meets the age requirement. The claimant lives in the UK, which meets the residency requirement. Although Alex does not live with the claimant, the claimant is contributing £26 per week consistently towards Alex's upkeep (clothes and school expenses). According to the rules, when a child lives with someone else, Child Benefit can continue beyond the initial 8-week period if the claimant contributes to the child's upkeep by at least the same amount as the Child Benefit payment. The current Child Benefit rate for one child is £25.60 per week (as of standard rates), and the claimant is contributing £26 per week, which meets or exceeds this threshold. Nobody else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Alex is not in hospital, care, or residential accommodation. Therefore, the claimant meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.
